target.description: Local MCP server launched over stdio.

metadata.instructions: Demo server used by mcpunit to regenerate the reports under `.reports/`.

tools.name: exec_command
tools.description: Execute an arbitrary shell command on the host machine.

command.description: Shell command to execute exactly as provided.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write text content to any file path on disk.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to write.

content.description: Content to write to the target file.

overwrite.description: Overwrite the file if it already exists.

tools.name: debug_payload
tools.description: Debug helper that accepts whatever input is available.

audit.why_this_score: Score is driven mainly by security findings in command execution and file system and ergonomics findings.
audit.methodology: Deterministic CI-first quality audit based on conformance, security-relevant capabilities, ergonomics, and metadata hygiene.
caveats: Low score means more deterministic findings or higher-risk exposed surface, not malicious intent.
High score means fewer deterministic findings, not a guarantee of safety.

rules.title: Duplicate tool names
rules.rationale: Tool names should be unique within one MCP server.

rules.title: Missing tool description
rules.rationale: Each tool should include a non-empty description.

rules.title: Overly generic tool name
rules.rationale: Tool names should communicate behavior clearly.

rules.title: Vague tool description
rules.rationale: Tool descriptions should clearly explain what the tool does.

rules.title: Missing schema type
rules.rationale: Tool input schemas should declare a top-level type.

rules.title: Schema allows arbitrary properties
rules.rationale: Tool input schemas should not allow arbitrary top-level properties.

rules.title: Weak input schema
rules.rationale: Tool input schemas should constrain free-form payloads clearly.

rules.title: Missing required critical fields
rules.rationale: Critical schema fields such as path, command, or URL should be required.

rules.title: Dangerous execution tool
rules.rationale: Tools that execute host shell commands are high risk.

rules.title: Dangerous download-and-execute tool
rules.rationale: Tools that combine remote download with shell execution are high risk.

rules.title: Dangerous filesystem write tool
rules.rationale: Tools that write files on disk are high risk.

rules.title: Dangerous filesystem delete tool
rules.rationale: Tools that delete files or directories are high risk.

rules.title: Dangerous HTTP request tool
rules.rationale: Tools that issue arbitrary HTTP requests are high risk.

rules.title: Dangerous network tool
rules.rationale: Tools that expose generic network access are high risk.

rules.title: Write tool without scope hint
rules.rationale: Filesystem mutation tools should document scope constraints clearly.

rules.title: Description mentions destructive access
rules.rationale: Tool descriptions should make destructive broad-scope access easy to spot.

rules.title: Response too large
rules.rationale: MCP servers should keep discovery responses small so agent contexts stay usable.

findings.title: Overly generic tool name
findings.rationale: Tool names should communicate behavior clearly.
findings.message: Tool 'do_it' uses an overly generic name that hides its behavior.

findings.title: Vague tool description
findings.rationale: Tool descriptions should clearly explain what the tool does.
findings.message: Tool 'do_it' uses a vague description that does not explain its behavior clearly.

findings.title: Schema allows arbitrary properties
findings.rationale: Tool input schemas should not allow arbitrary top-level properties.
findings.message: Tool 'debug_payload' allows arbitrary additional input properties.

findings.title: Weak input schema
findings.rationale: Tool input schemas should constrain free-form payloads clearly.
findings.message: Tool 'debug_payload' exposes a weak input schema that leaves free-form input underconstrained.

findings.title: Dangerous execution tool
findings.rationale: Tools that execute host shell commands are high risk.
findings.message: Tool 'exec_command' appears to expose host command execution.

findings.title: Dangerous filesystem write tool
findings.rationale: Tools that write files on disk are high risk.
findings.message: Tool 'write_file' appears to provide filesystem write access.

findings.title: Write tool without scope hint
findings.rationale: Filesystem mutation tools should document scope constraints clearly.
findings.message: Tool 'write_file' modifies the filesystem without any visible scope hint.
